Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Additive manufacturing Ti-22Al-25 Nb alloy with excellent high temperature tensile properties by electron beam powder bed fusion

Process_Text.original: Ti-22Al-25Nb powder ( Guangzhou Sailing Additive Manufacturing Co., Ltd, electrode induction melting gas atomization (EIGA), average particle size 59.03 um ) fabricated by Electron Beam Powder Bed Fusion (PBF-EB) on Sailong Y150 machine. Process parameters: Power 1050 W, Scanning speed 5.8 m/s, Hatch distance 0.11 mm, Layer thickness 50 um, Pre-heating temperature 830°C, Beam diameter 220 um. 90° layer rotation scanning strategy. High pre-heating temperature (near initial precipitation temperature of O-phase) designed to fully precipitate strip-shaped O-phase from B2 phase.
Process_Text.simplified: PBF-EB of Ti-22Al-25Nb using Sailong Y150 with 830°C pre-heating; Power 1050 W, scan speed 5.8 m/s, layer thickness 50 um

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxed beta phase grains with average diameter 46.5 um; O-phase (Ti2AlNb orthorhombic) area fraction 42.3% with average grain diameter > 0.5 um; strip-shaped O-phase dispersed in beta phase matrix; Al enriched in O-phase, Nb enriched in beta phase; weaker texture intensity; beta grains preferred growth along (001) direction; Burgers orientation relationships (100)O/(001)beta, (001)O/(110)beta, (010)O/(110)beta between O and beta phases.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed beta matrix with 42.3% O-phase precipitates (strip-shaped); uniform microstructure; weaker texture; O-beta orientation relationships maintained

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5e-4 s-1; horizontal direction
at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1e-3 s-1; 15 min dwell time before testing; Instron 5582 machine
at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5e-4 s-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: Room temperature tensile test; brittle fracture observed

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1e-3 s-1; 15 min dwell time before testing; Instron 5582 machine
Properties_Info.Note: Excellent high-temperature tensile strength; breakthrough improvement for TiAl alloys by AM; transgranular fracture observed

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5e-4 s-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: Room temperature tensile test; poor elongation; brittle fracture

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1e-3 s-1; 15 min dwell time before testing; Instron 5582 machine
Properties_Info.Note: Improved plasticity at high temperature with dimples on fracture surface

Process_Text.original: Ti-22Al-25Nb powder ( Guangzhou Sailing Additive Manufacturing Co., Ltd, electrode induction melting gas atomization (EIGA), average particle size 39.17 um ) fabricated by Laser Beam Powder Bed Fusion (PBF-LB) on Renishaw AM-400 machine. Process parameters: Power 140 W, Scanning speed 0.6 m/s, Hatch distance 0.12 mm, Layer thickness 30 um, Pre-heating temperature 170°C (maximum device temperature), Beam diameter 70 um. 67° layer rotation to reduce thermal stress.
Process_Text.simplified: PBF-LB of Ti-22Al-25Nb using Renishaw AM-400 with 170°C pre-heating; Power 140 W, scan speed 0.6 m/s, layer thickness 30 um

Microstructure_Text.original: Columnar beta phase grains with average diameter 67.2 um; O-phase (Ti2AlNb orthorhombic) area fraction only 7.34% with average grain diameter < 0.05 um; epitaxial growth of beta phase through molten pool boundaries; beta grains preferred growth along (101) and (111) directions; higher cooling rate (10^5-10^8 K/s) causes grain refinement of O-phase; Burgers orientation relationships between O and beta phases.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Columnar beta matrix with 7.34% fine O-phase precipitates; epitaxial grain growth along BD; strong texture along (101)/(111) directions

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5e-4 s-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: Room temperature tensile test; excellent tensile properties at RT with many dimples on fracture surface

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5e-4 s-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: Excellent room temperature tensile strength; ductile fracture with dimples

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1e-3 s-1; 15 min dwell time before testing; Instron 5582 machine
Properties_Info.Note: Poor high-temperature tensile strength at 650°C; rapid decrease in properties; brittle intergranular fracture

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5e-4 s-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: Excellent room temperature ductility; large number of dimples on fracture surface

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1e-3 s-1; 15 min dwell time before testing; Instron 5582 machine
Properties_Info.Note: Very poor elongation at high temperature; brittle fracture; intergranular fracture with cleavage facets
